name: Bengal Tiger
foundIn: West Bengal, Madhya Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Karnataka, Assam
description: Bengal Tiger is a majestic predator, known as India's national animal. It plays a critical role in maintaining ecosystem balance.
evolutionPattern: Developed striped camouflage for stalking prey in dense forests.
habitat: Mangrove forests (Sundarbans), grasslands, and tropical rainforests

name: Indian Elephant
foundIn: Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Assam, West Bengal, Odisha
threats: Habitat fragmentation, illegal ivory trade, human-wildlife conflict
description: The Indian Elephant is an intelligent and social animal, often found in herds. It plays a key role in shaping its environment and dispersing seeds.
evolutionPattern: Adapted for tropical forests; larger ears to dissipate heat.

name: Snow Leopard
foundIn: Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Sikkim
description: A solitary and elusive predator, the Snow Leopard is often called the Ghost of the Mountains.
evolutionPattern: Adapted to high-altitude cold climates with thick fur and large nasal cavities.

name: Asiatic Lion
foundIn: Gujarat (Gir Forest National Park only)
description: Found only in the Gir Forest, the Asiatic Lion is a symbol of resilience and conservation success.
evolutionPattern: Evolved to survive in tropical dry forests.

name: Gharial
foundIn: UP, MP, Bihar, Rajasthan — Chambal, Son, Gandak rivers
description: Known for its long, thin snout, the Gharial is a critically endangered crocodilian found in river ecosystems.
evolutionPattern: Adapted narrow snout for catching fish.

name: Indian Peacock
foundIn: Rajasthan, Gujarat, MP, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu
description: India's national bird, known for its iridescent blue-green plumage and elaborate courtship displays.
evolutionPattern: Evolved vibrant colors to attract mates and intimidate predators.

name: Indian Pangolin
foundIn: MP, Maharashtra, Odisha, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala
description: Covered in protective keratin scales, the Indian Pangolin is a shy nocturnal animal feeding on ants and termites.
evolutionPattern: Developed tough scales for defense against predators.

name: Indian Cobra
foundIn: Found across all states in India
description: A venomous snake revered in Indian culture, known for its iconic hood display.
evolutionPattern: Developed venom for hunting prey and defense.

name: Indian Star Tortoise
foundIn: Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Odisha
description: The Indian Star Tortoise is named for its star-like shell pattern and is often targeted for the illegal pet trade.
evolutionPattern: Adapted shell patterns for camouflage in dry grasslands.
habitat: Dry regions, scrub forests, and grasslands

name: African Elephant
foundIn: Botswana, South Africa, Kenya, Zimbabwe, Tanzania
description: The African Elephant is the largest land animal on Earth and plays an important ecological role.
evolutionPattern: Evolved large ears to dissipate heat in hot savanna climates.

name: Lion
foundIn: Kenya, Tanzania, South Africa, Botswana, Zimbabwe
description: Known as the king of the jungle, lions are apex predators key to the health of their habitat.
evolutionPattern: Evolved social pride structure for cooperative hunting.

name: Cheetah
foundIn: Namibia, South Africa, Botswana, Tanzania, Kenya
description: Cheetahs are the fastest land animals, known for their exceptional speed and slender bodies.
evolutionPattern: Evolved lightweight body and flexible spine for explosive speed.

name: Rhinoceros
foundIn: South Africa, Kenya, Namibia, Zimbabwe, Tanzania
description: Rhinos are large herbivores known for their thick skin and iconic horn.
evolutionPattern: Evolved thick skin as natural armor.

name: Giraffe
foundIn: Kenya, South Africa, Namibia, Zimbabwe, Tanzania
description: Giraffes are the tallest land animals, known for their long necks and distinct spotted coat patterns.
evolutionPattern: Evolved long neck to reach high foliage unavailable to other herbivores.

name: Kangaroo
foundIn: Throughout Australia
description: Iconic marsupials known for their powerful hind legs and pouch for carrying their young.
evolutionPattern: Evolved powerful hind legs for efficient movement across open terrain.

name: Koala
foundIn: Eastern and Southern Australia
description: Tree-dwelling marsupial known for its exclusive diet of eucalyptus leaves.
evolutionPattern: Evolved specialized digestive system to process toxic eucalyptus leaves.

name: Tasmanian Devil
foundIn: Tasmania
description: Carnivorous marsupial with strong jaws and black fur, threatened by a contagious facial tumor disease.
evolutionPattern: Evolved powerful jaws capable of crushing bone.
habitat: Forests and coastal scrublands of Tasmania

name: Kakapo
foundIn: Fiordland and offshore islands of New Zealand
description: Nocturnal, flightless parrot with green feathers. One of the world's rarest birds.
evolutionPattern: Lost flight ability due to historical lack of natural predators.
habitat: Dense forest on predator-free offshore islands

name: Kiwi
foundIn: North Island and South Island, New Zealand
description: Flightless bird and national symbol of New Zealand, known for its long beak and nocturnal habits.
evolutionPattern: Lost flight ability; evolved strong legs for ground foraging.

name: Jaguar
foundIn: Amazon Rainforest, Central and South America
description: Large feline known for its spotted coat and powerful build. Top predator in the Amazon.
evolutionPattern: Evolved powerful jaw to pierce turtle shells and caiman skulls.

name: Andean Condor
foundIn: Andes Mountains (Ecuador, Peru, Chile, Argentina)
description: One of the world's largest flying birds, with cultural importance across Andean civilisations.
evolutionPattern: Evolved to glide long distances using thermal updrafts in mountain terrain.

name: Magellanic Penguin
foundIn: Southern Patagonia (Chile, Argentina) and Falkland Islands
description: A medium-sized penguin known for its black-and-white plumage and coastal breeding colonies.
evolutionPattern: Developed strong swimming abilities for hunting fish in cold waters.

name: Maned Wolf
foundIn: Cerrado grasslands (Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay)
threats: Habitat loss, road collisions, diseases from domestic animals
description: A unique canid with long legs and a reddish coat, often called a fox on stilts.
evolutionPattern: Adapted for open grasslands with long legs for visibility and speed.

name: White-tailed Deer
foundIn: USA, Canada, Mexico, Central America
description: Known for their characteristic white under-tail, these deer are highly adaptable and thrive in various habitats.
evolutionPattern: Evolved white tail as an alarm signal to alert herd members of danger.

name: Moose
foundIn: North America, Northern Europe, Siberia
description: The largest species of deer, known for large palmate antlers and preference for cold climates.
evolutionPattern: Evolved large body mass to retain heat in cold environments.

name: Reindeer (Caribou)
foundIn: Arctic and Subarctic regions (Alaska, Canada, Scandinavia, Russia)
description: Famous for long migrations and cultural folklore, reindeer are iconic cold-climate mammals.
evolutionPattern: Evolved large hooves to act as snowshoes and paddles for swimming.
habitat: Tundra, boreal forests, and mountainous regions
